author: @secretbassrigs
text: FYI: the name of the song is "Malta" by Guerouabi

author: @Fr8gm3ntLives
text: Uses 4x the power generated to clean the panel lol

author: @grunwode
text: Very small objects, like clay, which is several orders of magnitude smaller than sand, are more dominated by their electrostatic surface properties than the physical properties of their mass.

author: @AlwaysCensored-xp1be
text: Can I use it on flyscreens to stop dust getting in the house?

author: @ecospider5
text: Solar farms use a lot of water to clean the panels. If this can reduce that it would be great

author: @QuintonNG2000
text: I would think a combination of vibration and angle would be more efficient. If your panel has to use most of irs output to keep itself clean its not doing much good

author: @Themachinewon
text: not sure where you live, but it doesn’t rain sand in my part

replies.author: @secretbassrigs
replies.text: the music is good. It's the reason I'm searching the comments for it's name.

author: @PașcaAlexandru
text: I was considering something like this as a dust repellant or even a lightning repellant but I fear that the electronics that follow the pv panels would not tolerate it well...

author: @StephenRansom47
text: 🤔 isn’t this what happened to the Solar Panels on the Mars Rovers? The mysterious removal of dust from the panels?

author: @AtlasReburdened
text: Problem us that it only works in the lab, with bone dry sand of a controlled grain size. Real outdoor air has a lot of dust and sticky organic molecules in it.

replies.author: @sub0fathom
replies.text: Have you considered that they have considered this and are testing it?

replies.author: @sub0fathom
replies.text:
@AtlasReburdened I am not making a claim. I am refuting your claim that "-it only works in the lab, [In which they have only tested it] with bone dry sand of a controlled grain size." 
I am not claiming they are doing anything for certain. I am only having you consider that you may not know for certain what they have and have not tried. In this case, you would be the one with the burden of providing the source for your original claim (stated above). I am claiming that neither of us know. I hope my original comment did not come across as aggressive or sarcastic. I meant to only be friendly. Something that is difficult to communicate online.

replies.author: @AtlasReburdened
replies.text:
"Have you considered that *_they have considered this and are testing it._*
I put the claim in bold and italics for greater ease of recognition.

replies.author: @sub0fathom
replies.text: @AtlasReburdened I apologize for the confusion. I am not claiming that I know they have considered it. I am asking if you have considered it as a hypothetical possibility.

author: @Michiganmoose69
text: That would work great on all of my dusty furniture
